teamObjective.explanation: Partner (player 2) has only 2 cards remaining and is near finishing. My hand contains only low non-trump diamonds, so the best team use of the opening lead is to play a card that partner is most likely able to beat, transferring the lead so partner can control the next trick and attempt to exit.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner likely has a small set of remaining cards that may include a high single or trump needed to finish. With just 2 cards, partner is probably trying to win a trick and then lead the final card. I should not assume specific ranks or suits, only that partner's remaining hand is small and likely geared toward exiting.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents each hold 3 cards and no cards have been played, so they likely possess some higher non-trump cards and possibly trump cards. They will try to capture the lead to block partner from finishing, but with no public information I cannot assign specific cards.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Among my four low diamond singles, the 3 is the weakest. Leading the 3 gives partner the widest range of possible winning replies (any higher non-trump or trump single) and preserves my slightly larger diamonds for later if needed.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Leading the 6 is stronger but still a low non-trump card. Opponents are more likely to be able to beat it, and partner may not have a card above 6, so this reduces the chance of transferring control.
The 4 is better than the 6 but still harder for partner to beat than the 3, and it does not preserve as much strength as keeping the 4-6 intact.
Similar to the 4 and 6, the 5 gives partner a narrower winning range than the 3 while being more vulnerable to opponents.

risks: An opponent may beat the 3 with a higher card or trump, preventing partner from gaining the lead.
Partner may not have a winning reply to even a 3, in which case control passes to an opponent anyway.
riskAssessment.mitigation: No lead from this weak hand can guarantee partner control, but the 3 minimizes the strength required from partner and is the most lead-transfer-friendly option available.
